page: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The CPU allocation is fully dedicated, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 24 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI, alongside AES cryptography and BMI2 integer manipulation flags.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average range across most standardized tests. It achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1574.48 and a multi-core score of 8306.94. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block bandwidth of 463.15 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 39.71 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the instance processes 8.05 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers a static web serving throughput of 7.07 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is relatively weak, scoring 30.85 GB/sec, which represents a potential bottleneck for highly parallelized decompression tasks.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c2-standard-8 offers dedicated compute resources without the added expense of bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, making it a streamlined option for CPU-bound workloads. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a balanced memory footprint for compute-heavy tasks. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and light language model inference, as demonstrated by its 127.16 tokens/sec text generation speed on a 135M parameter model.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-8 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture. It provides 4 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 24 MB L3 cache, and support for AVX-512 instructions. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average performance across most workloads, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 8306.94, a Redis SET throughput of 8.05 million operations per second, and a static web serving speed of 7.07 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative weak point. This instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and web serving where dedicated CPU performance is required without the overhead of local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-8 is a compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a dedicated 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, designed for compute-heavy workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-8 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a dedicated 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and AVX-512 support for high-throughput CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM with a dedicated 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Supports AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI advanced instruction sets
Delivers 8.05 million Redis SET operations per second
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
